catalog: ActionC64U Idun language and runtime help

topics.summary: Starts another group of global declarations.
topics.details: Bare MODULE follows the original Action syntax; an optional name is accepted for readable binding files. The project manifest and source filename determine the emitted object module.

topics.summary: Begins a procedure that does not return a value.
topics.details: Parameters may be BYTE, CARD, INT, record, ARRAY, or POINTER forms; source-language routines also accept REAL. A fixed address declares a resident machine-language routine; =* starts an inline code routine. Machine-routine argument bytes are flattened into A, X, Y, then $A3 upward. Procedure names are assignable, matching Action's redirectable entry semantics. MAIN may be parameterless or use PROC MAIN(CARD argc,CARD ARRAY argv) to receive Idun's process arguments. argc includes the executable name; argv contains pointers to zero-terminated strings.

topics.summary: Explicitly ends the current procedure.
topics.details: The terminator is optional before another routine declaration, but using it makes routine boundaries clear.

topics.summary: Begins a typed function that returns a value.
topics.details: Action functions use RETURN(expression); an =* machine-code function may return directly from an inline code block. BYTE, CARD, and INT return in A/X (BYTE uses A and is zero-extended); REAL returns through a function-owned four-byte result cell. Machine/external REAL routines are not supported by the target ABI.

topics.summary: Explicitly ends the current function.
topics.details: The next routine declaration also closes a function, but ENDFUNC is the clearest spelling.

topics.summary: Returns from the current procedure or function.
topics.details: Procedures use bare RETURN. Typed functions use RETURN(expression) of a compatible type.

topics.summary: Declares an unsigned 8-bit value.
topics.details: BYTE values range from 0 to 255. BYTE arrays also represent Action strings, with the length in element zero.

topics.summary: Declares an 8-bit character value.
topics.details: CHAR is fully interchangeable with BYTE, as in the original Action language. Character constants use a leading apostrophe, for example 'A.

topics.summary: Declares an unsigned 16-bit cardinal value.
topics.details: CARD values range from 0 to 65535 and are also used for C64 addresses passed to runtime helpers.

topics.summary: Declares a signed 16-bit value or converts REAL to INT.
topics.details: INT values range from -32768 to 32767. Multiplication, division, and MOD use Action's signed 16-bit result semantics. INT(realExpression) truncates toward zero and returns zero for NaN, infinity, or a truncated result outside the INT range.

topics.summary: Declares a four-byte REAL value or converts an integer to REAL.
topics.details: REAL is IEEE-754 binary32: signed finite values, signed zero, gradual-underflow subnormals, infinity, and NaN. Arithmetic and FSqrt round to nearest with ties to even. Comparisons are IEEE ordered comparisons; NaN is unequal to every value, including itself. Decimal constants round to binary32 after every operation.
topics.target_notes: REAL occupies four little-endian bytes. The standalone 6502 helpers cover the full binary32 value range; INT conversion is constrained only by the signed 16-bit destination type.

topics.summary: References a compiler-validated graphics file without putting its pixel bytes in Action source.
topics.details: ACTC searches beside the source, in project RES, and at the project root. Sprite files are compiled to aligned VIC-II blocks; bitmap files retain a compact ABM1 header used by GFX1 blitters. In ACTEDIT, F8 on the declaration launches the matching graphics editor.

topics.summary: Declares an aligned 24-by-21 high-resolution sprite resource.
topics.details: ACTSPRITE stores an ASP1 editing file. ACTC embeds its 63 VIC-II pattern bytes plus default-color metadata and exposes the resource name as a CARD address.

topics.summary: Declares an aligned 12-by-21 logical multicolor sprite resource.
topics.details: Each logical pixel has a value from zero through three. SpritePlace reads the embedded mode/default color; SpriteSharedColors configures the two colors shared by all VIC-II multicolor sprites.

topics.summary: Declares a packed one-bit bitmap resource up to 320 by 200 pixels.
topics.details: ACTBITMAP edits the ABM1 file; ACTC validates dimensions, stride, mode, colors, and payload before embedding it. BitmapStamp treats zero pixels as transparent.

topics.summary: Declares a packed two-bit multicolor bitmap resource up to 160 by 200 logical pixels.
topics.details: Each logical pixel selects color slot zero through three. MBitmapStamp treats slot zero as transparent; MBitmapDrawResource draws every slot.

topics.summary: The positive IEEE-754 REAL infinity literal.
topics.details: Use -INF for negative infinity. Finite overflow and nonzero division by signed zero also produce signed infinity.

topics.summary: An unordered IEEE-754 REAL not-a-number literal.
topics.details: Invalid arithmetic such as 0.0/0.0, INF-INF, or FSqrt(-1.0) produces NaN. Every ordered comparison with NaN is false; <> is true.

topics.summary: Declares contiguous typed storage or an array-address cell.
topics.details: Subscripts are zero based and are not bounds checked on the C64. A dimension allocates storage; an undimensioned array is a pointer cell. An initializer determines the allocated element count, matching original Action even when the written dimension differs; (0) is therefore useful with inferred initializers. BYTE arrays may use quoted Action-string initializers. @array(index) obtains an element address.

topics.summary: Declares a typed 16-bit C64 address.
topics.details: Use @variable to obtain an address and pointer^ to read or write the pointed-to value. The pointed-to type determines the transfer width.

topics.summary: Declares a typed compile-time constant.
topics.details: Constants may refer to earlier constants and are range checked for their declared type. Multiple comma-separated declarations and continued declaration lines are supported.

topics.summary: Defines a compile-time token substitution.
topics.details: Replacement is recursive, case-insensitive, and skipped inside strings and comments. Original quoted replacements and compatible unquoted replacements are accepted; definitions apply through nested includes.

topics.summary: Compiles another Action source file at this point.
topics.details: Includes may nest and definitions persist in source order. ACTC searches the including directory, project SRC and LIB, then the shared workspace LIB, using case-insensitive filename matching and cycle checks.

topics.summary: Accepts an original Action compile-time setting directive.
topics.details: SET is parsed and validated for source compatibility. The old cartridge compiler's RAM option addresses do not exist in the Linux compiler, so those memory pokes intentionally have no effect.
topics.target_notes: Use Idun build options rather than Atari cartridge compiler-memory addresses.

topics.summary: Declares a packed record type.
topics.details: Record instances may use compiler-owned storage or an absolute C64 address. Record values and record pointers may be routine parameters. Record pointers and instance.field references preserve packed field offsets.

topics.summary: Emits raw 6502 bytes and words into a routine.
topics.details: Values through 255 emit one byte; wider constants and symbol addresses emit little-endian words. Adjacent hexadecimal constants may be packed without spaces, as in original Action. Symbol-plus-constant relocations and the current code address * are supported. No instruction validation is performed.
topics.example: [$A9 7 $60]  ; packed words such as [$FFA2$A686] are also valid

topics.summary: Assembles validated NMOS 6502 instructions inside an Action routine.
topics.details: The assembler supports all legal NMOS 6502 opcodes and addressing modes, forward and backward block-local labels, relative branch checks, and absolute JSR/JMP relocation. Operands may name fixed C64 memory, Action globals, locals, normal procedure/function parameters, REAL storage, records, and declared routines. Use symbol+offset for a later byte and #<symbol or #>symbol for a relocated address byte. CPU registers are used through ordinary instructions such as LDA, TAX, and DEX. Labels are scoped to one ASMBLOCK.
topics.example:
ASMBLOCK [
    lda count
    clc
    adc amount
    sta count
loop:
    dex
    bne loop
]
topics.target_notes: This emits C64 6502 code; it is not Linux host assembly. Parameters of ordinary Action routines have named storage. A =* machine routine receives its legacy flattened ABI directly in A, X, Y, and $A3 upward.

topics.summary: Combines conditions with short-circuit logical AND.
topics.details: The right condition is evaluated only when the left condition is true. Parentheses may group complex conditions.

topics.summary: Combines conditions with short-circuit logical OR.
topics.details: The right condition is evaluated only when the left condition is false. AND binds more tightly than OR.

topics.summary: Computes a bitwise exclusive OR.
topics.details: XOR and ! are equivalent Action operators and operate over the active 16-bit expression value.

topics.summary: Returns the signed 16-bit division remainder.
topics.details: MOD follows Action's implied INT semantics; a negative dividend produces a negative remainder and division truncates toward zero.

topics.summary: Shifts a 16-bit value left.
topics.details: Both constant and runtime shift counts are supported. Bits shifted beyond the 16-bit result are discarded.

topics.summary: Shifts a 16-bit value right without sign extension.
topics.details: RSH matches Action's logical shift behavior even for INT values.

topics.summary: Allocates a far BYTE array in C64 REU memory.
topics.details: The declaration stores an allocator handle in the named 16-bit value. Access the block with ReuPeek8/16 and ReuPoke8/16.
topics.target_notes: Requires working REU-compatible hardware. Free, copy, and 32-bit operations are not active.

topics.summary: Begins a named program-owned code section.
topics.details: Call the section with OverlayCall(name). ALINK currently places it resident in the same PRG; dynamic loading and unloading are not active.

topics.summary: Ends the current overlay section.
topics.details: The section is linked as resident code in the current Idun toolchain.

topics.summary: Begins a conditional branch.
topics.details: Conditions support =, <>, #, !=, <, <=, >, and >=. Close the branch with FI and optionally add ELSEIF or ELSE clauses.

topics.summary: Ends an IF or ELSEIF condition and begins its body.
topics.details: THEN is required on IF and ELSEIF lines.

topics.summary: Adds another tested branch to an IF block.
topics.details: ELSEIF is tested only when all earlier branches in the same IF block were false.

topics.summary: Adds the fallback branch of an IF block.
topics.details: ELSE must be the final branch before FI.

topics.summary: Ends an IF/ELSEIF/ELSE block.
topics.details: Every active IF block must close with FI.

topics.summary: Begins a counted loop.
topics.details: Initial value, final bound, and STEP expression are evaluated once on entry. STEP defaults to 1 and must evaluate to a nonzero value; negative steps count downward. Later changes to variables used by the bound or step do not alter the active loop. Close with OD.
topics.example: FOR i=first TO last STEP stride

topics.summary: Separates a FOR loop's initial and final values.
topics.details: The final expression is evaluated once when entering the loop.

topics.summary: Sets a FOR loop's increment or decrement.
topics.details: The expression is captured once when the loop starts and must evaluate to a nonzero value. A negative result creates a descending loop.

topics.summary: Begins a pre-tested loop.
topics.details: The condition is tested before each iteration. Close the loop with OD.

topics.summary: Begins an unconditional loop.
topics.details: Use EXIT to leave the nearest loop and OD to mark its end.

topics.summary: Ends a post-tested loop when its condition becomes true.
topics.details: The body executes before the condition is tested. UNTIL supplies the post-test and the loop then closes with OD.

topics.summary: Ends a FOR, WHILE, or DO loop.
topics.details: Control returns to the loop's counter update or condition test.

topics.summary: Leaves the nearest active loop.
topics.details: EXIT is valid inside FOR, WHILE, DO, and compatible post-tested loop bodies.

topics.summary: Prints an Action length-prefixed string followed by a carriage return.
topics.details: Element zero supplies the length and following elements supply characters. Output goes directly through the C64 KERNAL CHROUT routine.

topics.summary: Prints a signed 16-bit integer without a trailing newline.
topics.details: The linker selects the standalone integer formatting helper only when the program uses it.

topics.summary: Prints a signed 16-bit integer followed by a carriage return.
topics.details: It uses the same formatter as PrintI and then writes CR through C64 KERNAL CHROUT.

topics.summary: Calls a named OVERLAY section.
topics.details: The name is resolved by ALINK. In the current implementation the section is resident in the generated PRG.

libraries.name: MATH1
libraries.details: MATH1 combines the full-domain IEEE-754 binary32 core with portable Action implementations of rounding, exponential, logarithmic, circular trigonometric, reciprocal trigonometric, hyperbolic, and angle-conversion functions. Angles are radians unless the function name says otherwise.
libraries.target_notes: Core arithmetic, FAbs, FSqrt, and FTrunc use link-selected 6502 runtime modules. Higher functions use binary32 polynomial/range-reduction code from MATH1.ACT and therefore have binary32 approximation error, especially for very large trigonometric arguments.

topics.summary: The binary32 approximation of pi.
The binary32 approximation of two pi.
The binary32 approximation of pi divided by two.
The binary32 approximation of pi divided by four.
The binary32 approximation of Euler's number.
The binary32 approximation of the natural logarithm of two.
The binary32 approximation of the natural logarithm of ten.
The binary32 approximation of the square root of two.
Prints the exact decimal value without a trailing newline.
topics.details: Finite values are expanded exactly, without a magnitude or fractional-digit cutoff. Signed zero prints as 0 or -0; special values print as INF, -INF, or NAN.

topics.summary: Prints the exact decimal value followed by a carriage return.
topics.details: It uses the same full-domain exact formatter as PrintR and then writes CR through C64 KERNAL CHROUT.

topics.summary: Returns the absolute value of a REAL expression.
Returns the correctly rounded binary32 square root.
topics.details: Positive finite values use round-to-nearest, ties-to-even. Signed zero is preserved, positive infinity remains infinity, and a negative nonzero operand produces NAN.

topics.summary: Returns -1, signed zero, 1, or NAN according to the operand.
Returns the smaller numeric operand, ignoring one NAN operand.
Returns the larger numeric operand, ignoring one NAN operand.
Constrains a value to an inclusive REAL interval.
Removes the fractional part toward zero and returns a REAL.
topics.details: The link-selected helper preserves signed zero, infinities, NaN payloads, and values that are already integral.

topics.summary: Rounds downward to an integral REAL value.
Rounds upward to an integral REAL value.
Rounds to the nearest integral REAL with halfway cases away from zero.
Returns the signed fractional part of a REAL value.
Returns a floating remainder with the dividend's sign.
Returns e raised to a REAL power.
Returns the natural logarithm of a positive REAL value.
Returns the base-two logarithm.
Returns the base-ten logarithm.
Raises a REAL base to a REAL exponent.
topics.details: A negative base requires an integral exponent; otherwise the result is NAN.

topics.summary: Computes a scaled square root of x squared plus y squared.
Returns the sine of an angle in radians.
Returns the cosine of an angle in radians.
Returns the tangent of an angle in radians.
Returns the inverse sine in the interval -pi/2 through pi/2.
Returns the inverse cosine in the interval zero through pi.
Returns the inverse tangent in the interval -pi/2 through pi/2.
Returns the quadrant-aware angle of a vector.
Returns the secant, one divided by cosine.
Returns the cosecant, one divided by sine.
Returns the cotangent, cosine divided by sine.
Returns the inverse secant.
Returns the inverse cosecant.
Returns the inverse cotangent using quadrant-aware atan2.
Returns one minus the cosine of an angle.
Returns half the versine, useful in great-circle calculations.
Returns the hyperbolic sine.
Returns the hyperbolic cosine.
Returns the hyperbolic tangent.
Returns the inverse hyperbolic sine.
Returns the inverse hyperbolic cosine for values at least one.
Returns the inverse hyperbolic tangent for values from -1 through 1.
Converts degrees to radians.
Converts radians to degrees.

libraries.name: REU
libraries.details: These helpers resolve a REU allocation handle plus a 16-bit byte offset and then transfer directly through the C64 REU registers.
libraries.target_notes: Invalid handles and out-of-range reads return zero; invalid writes leave REU memory unchanged. REU hardware is required.

topics.summary: Reads one byte from an allocated REU block.
Reads a little-endian 16-bit value from an allocated REU block.
Writes one byte to an allocated REU block.
Writes a little-endian 16-bit value to an allocated REU block.

libraries.name: GFX1
libraries.details: GFX1 combines link-selected VIC-II helpers with clipped pixel, shape, sprite-resource, and bitmap-resource operations. High-resolution coordinates are 320 by 200; multicolor coordinates are logically 160 by 200.
libraries.target_notes: Large programs should use VIC bank 2 with screen RAM at $8400, sprite staging at $8800, and bitmap RAM at $A000. The tracked high-level calls safely expose RAM beneath BASIC ROM. SPRITE resources compile to aligned 64-byte blocks; BITMAP resources retain their validated ABM1 header and packed pixels.

Selects and records the bitmap-memory base for high-level drawing.
Selects the 64-byte-aligned VIC-bank destination where SpritePlace stages slots zero through seven.
Configures tracked bank, screen, and bitmap addresses and enables hires bitmap mode.
Configures tracked bank, screen, and bitmap addresses and enables multicolor bitmap mode.
Fills the tracked 8000-byte bitmap, including RAM hidden beneath BASIC ROM.

libraries.name: INPUT1
libraries.details: INPUT1 reads C64 joystick and 1351-style mouse state. Port values use normal C64 port numbers 1 or 2.
libraries.target_notes: An idle passive joystick cannot be reliably detected. Mouse presence is inferred only after movement or button activity.

topics.summary: Joystick up-direction bit mask.
Joystick down-direction bit mask.
Joystick left-direction bit mask.
Joystick right-direction bit mask.
Primary joystick-button bit mask.
Optional second joystick-button bit mask.
Primary mouse-button bit mask.
Secondary mouse-button bit mask.
Polls a joystick and returns the active direction/button mask.
Reports whether activity has been observed on a joystick port.
Returns nonzero while the primary joystick button is pressed.
Returns nonzero while the supported second joystick button is pressed.
Polls mouse position/buttons and updates the linked mouse state.
Reports whether mouse movement or button activity has been observed.
Returns the most recently polled mouse X value.
Returns the most recently polled mouse Y value.
Returns the current combined mouse-button mask.
Returns nonzero while mouse button 1 is pressed.
Returns nonzero while mouse button 2 is pressed.

libraries.name: SIDSPR1
libraries.details: SIDSPR1 calls directly control VIC-II sprites and SID voices through link-selected 6502 helpers. Sprite numbers are 0 through 7 and SID voice numbers are 0 through 2.
libraries.target_notes: Callers are responsible for valid sprite data placement, VIC bank setup, and musically appropriate SID register values.

libraries.name: DBF1
libraries.details: DBF1 operates on the currently staged DBF image in an allocator-owned REU block. Filename arguments point to zero-terminated C64 strings; explicit DbfSave writes changes back through C64 KERNAL device 8 I/O.
libraries.target_notes: The current implementation supports one staged DBF image up to 65535 bytes. Its generated 6502 REU/KERNAL path is proven in headless VICE with a D64 image; physical Idun/C64/REU validation remains pending.

topics.summary: Creates and opens an empty DBF image for the supplied C64 filename.
Loads a DBF file into REU storage and returns its handle.
Closes the staged DBF handle and releases its active adapter state.
Moves the current-record cursor to a record number.
Returns the number of fields described by the DBF header.
Returns one field's byte length.
Reads a raw byte at an offset in the current record.

topics.signature: BYTE FUNC DbfReadFieldByte(BYTE handle,BYTE field,BYTE offset)
topics.summary: Reads a byte relative to a field in the current record.

topics.signature: BYTE FUNC DbfWriteFieldByte(BYTE handle,BYTE field,BYTE offset,BYTE value)
topics.summary: Writes a byte relative to a field in the current record.

topics.signature: BYTE FUNC DbfWriteByte(BYTE handle,BYTE offset,BYTE value)
topics.summary: Writes a raw byte at an offset in the current record.
Appends a blank record and selects it as current.
Removes records marked deleted and compacts the staged image.
Writes the staged DBF image back to its filename.
Marks the current record as deleted.
Clears the deleted marker on the current record.
Reports whether the current record is marked deleted.

Returns the byte length of each DBF record.
